$comment: Tool declarations for the go ratchet. go_build is the oracle: it type-checks Go source from stdin with `go build`. The engine dispatches the .sh script to bash by extension; on Windows add a go_check.ps1 and a second tool entry (or swap the script field).

tools.name: go_build
tools.description: Type-check a complete Go source file with `go build` (as a library package) and report OK or the build diagnostics. The Go source is supplied on stdin.

tools.name: go_test
tools.description: BEHAVIOR oracle: type-check, `go vet`, and `go test` a Go package. The payload (on stdin) carries two files introduced by markers: `// === solution.go ===` (the implementation, `package solution`) and `// === solution_test.go ===` (the tests). Exit 0 iff vet is clean and the tests pass. Requires a *_test.go file (a package with no tests is a silent false pass).

code.description: two marker-separated Go files: solution.go and solution_test.go

tools.name: doctor_go
tools.description: Toolbelt probe for `ratchet doctor` (analog of cpp's doctor_cl): reports the Go toolbelt and its optional linters. Exit 0 if the required `go` toolchain (build/vet/test) is present, 1 if not. Optional linters are reported as info, never block. Run directly with `/do doctor_go`.
